free_text: Stellar krypton cross sections at kT = 25 and 52 keV
width and 2 muA beam current during 52 keV spectrum
kT = 25 keV shape. 1.911 MeV protons on metallic Li-7
layer with copper backing of 0.5 mm. Kinematically
collimated neutron beam forming a 120 degree cone.
Maximum neutron energy of 106 keV. Neutron scattering
on the Cu backing was neglected with uncertainty
Used for approximation to Maxwellian kT = 52keV
shape. 1099 keV protons on tritium loaded titanium
layers (1.0 mg/cm2) evaporated on 0.3 mm thick
tungsten disk, covered by a aluminium layer of
50 ug/cm2. Specific target activity was 3.0E10 bq/cm2.
Cooling water flows in 0.8 mm gap between W backing
and 0.1 mm outer stainless steel window.
120 degree cone. Max neutron energy 225 keV for
kT = 52 keV. Transmission of target at 30 keV only
89% owing to n-p scattering in cooling water
leading to additional uncertainty in cross section
The neutron spectra are closely approximated to max-
wellian shapes with temperatures of 25 and 52 keV.
Natural and enriched Kr trapped in zeolite 5A crystal
lattice, typically 50 cm3 krypton per gram zeolite,
sandwiched between gold foils of same diameter 6 mm.
Gamma-ray self absorption in sample, background,
Ge(Li) detector efficiency, neutron flux dispersion
deviation of flux from Maxwellian shape.
Uncertainty in gamma ray self absorption
